mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions and consumer spending power, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns, but only during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction during stable policy periods, yielding negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust employment growth, which supports consumer income and corporate profit margins, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 across rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond 3.0 percent, it forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to predict negative returns, with positive 60-day outcomes occurring in over 60 percent of high-inflation months, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal weakening manufacturing activity and falling capacity utilization, which triggers immediate profit downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns, but only during late-cycle economic phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of late-cycle downturns, or the predictive accuracy (Rank IC) falls below 0.02 in regime-specific out-of-sample tests.
